articles.content:
World News Today Live Updates: In today’s rapidly changing world, staying updated with global news is essential. Our World News coverage offers comprehensive insights into key international events, from politics and economics to social movements and cultural shifts. Get the latest updates on geopolitical developments, policy changes, economic trends, and significant global issues that shape societies and influence lives across continents. With expert analysis and timely reporting, we bring you a clear view of the forces impacting today’s global landscape, keeping you informed and prepared. Explore stories that reveal how international events connect and affect communities worldwide, ensuring you never miss a moment.

This is an AI-generated live blog and has not been edited by Live Mint staff.

For about a decade, Livemint—News Desk has been a credible source for authentic and timely news, and well-researched analysis on national news, business, personal finance, corporates, politics and geopolitics. We bring the latest updates on all the listed companies on BSE and NSE, startups, mutual funds, Union ministries, geopolitics, and untapped human interest stories from around the world, helping our readers to stay informed on the latest developments around the globe. Our Coverage Areas 1. Companies: Comprehensive news and analysis on listed and unlisted companies, corporate announcements, corporate chatter, C-suite, business trends, hiring alerts, layoffs, work-life balance, world's top billionaires and richest and more. 2. Personal finance: Insights into mutual funds, small savings schemes like - PPF, SSY, post office savings scheme, stock to watch, personal loans, credit cards, top bank FDs, real estate, income tax and more. 3. Politics: Comprehensive coverage of general elections, state elections and bypolls, Lok Sabha, Vidhan Sabha, Parliament, PMO, PIB, finance ministry, home ministry, among other union ministries and government departments. 4. National News: From metro cities like Delhi, Mumbai, and e to untapped stories from rural India, we cover human interest, health, education, crime and courts, and law and order, among other areas of public interest. 5. Economy: In-depth analysis of India's macro and micro-economic indicators like- GDP, inflation, forex, fiscal deficit, current account deficit, interest rate cycle, economic recovery, RBI circulars, indirect taxes, GST, Insolvency and Bankruptcy imports, exports and everything that impacts Indian economy. 6. Geopolitics: Well-rounded and deeply researched coverage on US News, Oval Office European Union, Ukraine Russia War, middle-east crisis, royal families and global leaders like - Donald Trump, Vladimir Putin, Kim Jong Un, Xi Jinping and premiers of other leading economies in the world. articles.title: Unilever PLC ADR steady as investors weigh consumer staples exposure
articles.summary: The Unilever PLC ADR (UL) remains a key defensive investment for US investors, offering exposure to the global consumer goods group amidst inflation, pricing, and demand fluctuations. The company's diverse portfolio of food, home, and personal care brands, coupled with its con...
articles.content:
Unilever PLC (ADR) UL (ISIN GB00B10RZP78) represents US investors' access to the global consumer goods group behind many everyday household and personal care brands. The ADR structure lets US holders gain exposure to a London-listed staple, often seen as a defensive holding during periods of economic uncertainty. As a large consumer staples issuer, the company tends to attract investors looking for steady cash flows, diversified geographic exposure and resilience against short-term market swings.

Global consumer staples footprint

Unilever PLC operates a broad portfolio of food, home care and personal care products that are sold in markets worldwide, including North America, Europe, Asia and emerging economies. Its brands typically occupy leading positions in key categories such as detergents, soaps, skincare, hair care and packaged foods, which helps the group defend market share even in competitive environments. This global footprint provides diversification across currencies and economies, which can appeal to investors who want to reduce reliance on any single region.

The ADR UL trades in the US over-the-counter market, giving access to Unilever PLC's London primary listing without requiring investors to trade directly on a foreign exchange. The underlying company reports in its home currency and follows reporting and governance standards applicable to its UK listing, while ADR holders receive translated information through depositary bank structures and regulatory filings. For many US investors, this can be a practical route into international consumer staples exposure.

Earnings, dividends and cash generation

As a mature consumer goods group, Unilever PLC's investment case often centers on its ability to generate consistent cash flow and to return part of that cash to shareholders via dividends and, where appropriate, share buybacks. Over recent years, the company has typically reported steady revenue driven by a combination of volume growth and pricing actions, especially during times of higher input costs and inflation. Investors tend to follow how management balances price increases with maintaining volumes and brand loyalty, as this dynamic can affect both margins and long-term growth.

Dividend policy is another focal point for UL ADR holders. Global consumer staples groups are often favored for their regular dividend payments, which can provide income in addition to potential capital appreciation. Unilever PLC has historically signaled a commitment to shareholder returns through its payout practices, subject to board decisions and overall financial performance. For US investors, dividends from the ADR are received in US dollars, with currency translation effects reflecting movements between the home currency and USD.

Brand portfolio and business model

Unilever PLC's business model centers on building, marketing and distributing branded consumer products across a wide range of categories and price points. The group invests heavily in advertising and product innovation to reinforce brand recognition and to differentiate its offerings from private-label and smaller competitors. Distribution involves partnerships with retailers, wholesalers and e-commerce platforms, with an increasing focus on digital channels as consumer purchasing habits evolve.

The product mix spans premium, mid-range and value segments, enabling the company to address different consumer budgets and preferences. In periods of economic strain, value and mainstream brands may gain relative importance, while in more favorable environments, premium personal care and beauty lines can drive higher-margin growth. This layered portfolio gives management flexibility to tilt investment and promotional spending toward the segments showing the strongest demand at any given time.

Sustainability and social responsibility have become key pillars of Unilever PLC's strategy. The company has articulated ambitions around reducing environmental impact, improving packaging recyclability, lowering emissions and supporting fair labor practices across its supply chain. These themes can matter to a growing segment of investors who integrate environmental, social and governance considerations into their portfolio decisions. For UL ADR holders, understanding these initiatives can help gauge how the company aligns with long-term consumer preferences and regulatory trends.

Representative product example

One widely recognized example from Unilever PLC's portfolio is Dove, a personal care brand that includes soaps, body wash, shampoos and skincare products. Dove has been positioned around themes of real beauty and gentle care, aiming to build emotional connections with consumers in addition to offering functional benefits. Marketing campaigns often highlight everyday users rather than traditional models, reflecting a strategy to appeal to broad demographics and to support brand loyalty.

Dove illustrates how Unilever PLC leverages a single brand across multiple product categories and geographies. The brand appears in grocery stores, drugstores and mass-market retailers, as well as on online platforms. By extending Dove into related personal care categories, the company can capture more shelf space and cross-selling opportunities while benefiting from shared marketing investment. For investors, such flagship brands represent intangible assets that can support pricing power and resilience even amid intense competition.

UL ADR trading context

The UL ADR allows US investors to trade Unilever PLC exposure in US dollars and within US market infrastructure. Trading in the ADR reflects movements in the underlying London-listed shares, adjusted for the ADR ratio and currency effects. As with other large multinational consumer goods issuers, daily price moves in UL can be influenced by global macroeconomic data, changes in consumer spending patterns, foreign exchange swings and sector-wide sentiment rather than solely company-specific news.

articles.title: VBF and the Invesco Bond Fund. A closer look at fixed income exposure
articles.summary: This article provides an in-depth look at the Invesco Bond Fund (VBF, ISIN US92340E1091), explaining its structure, investment strategy, and how it offers diversified fixed income exposure. It details how the fund manages interest rate and credit risk, and how its portfolio ma...
articles.content:
VBF, associated with the Invesco Bond Fund (ISIN US92340E1091), gives investors access to a professionally managed portfolio of fixed income securities. The fund is designed to offer diversified exposure to bonds across issuers, sectors, and maturities, aiming for income generation and capital preservation within the broader debt market.

How the Invesco Bond Fund is structured

The Invesco Bond Fund typically invests in a mix of government, corporate, and other debt instruments to build a diversified bond portfolio. Managers allocate capital across different maturities and credit qualities to balance yield, duration, and risk. This mix helps spread exposure across sectors and issuers so that performance does not depend on a single bond or borrower.

Bond funds like this generally seek a combination of income and total return. Income comes primarily from coupon payments on the underlying bonds, while total return also reflects market price movements as interest rates and credit spreads change over time. The fund's managers regularly adjust holdings to reflect updated views on interest rates, credit risk, and relative value in the bond market.

Interest rates, duration, and risk focus

One of the central drivers of performance for the Invesco Bond Fund is the interest rate environment. When benchmark rates rise, existing bonds with lower coupons often trade at discounts, pressuring net asset value; when rates fall, those same bonds can appreciate, supporting returns. Duration, a measure of interest rate sensitivity, is therefore a key metric for understanding how the fund might respond to rate moves.

Credit risk also plays a core role. The fund's exposure to investment-grade and possibly some higher-yielding bonds affects both income potential and volatility. Higher-yielding bonds can offer more coupon income but carry greater default risk and price variability. By diversifying across issuers and industries, the fund aims to reduce the impact of any single credit event while still capturing an attractive yield profile for investors.

Investment strategy and portfolio management

The Invesco Bond Fund follows a systematic process for selecting and monitoring bonds in its portfolio. Managers typically start with a top-down view of macroeconomic trends, including growth, inflation, and central bank policy, to decide on overall duration, sector allocation, and interest rate positioning. They then combine this with bottom-up analysis of individual issuers, examining balance sheets, cash flows, and covenant structures to evaluate creditworthiness.

Sector allocation is another important piece of the strategy. Exposure to sectors such as financials, industrials, utilities, and sovereign issuers influences both income and risk. The fund may tilt toward sectors that offer better relative value or stronger fundamentals, while reducing exposure to areas where spreads do not compensate for perceived risks. This dynamic allocation allows the portfolio to adapt as market conditions evolve.

Liquidity management is crucial in bond funds, especially under volatile market conditions. Portfolio managers aim to maintain a mix of highly liquid securities and more specialized holdings so that the fund can meet redemption requests and reposition the portfolio without incurring excessive transaction costs. This balancing act affects how quickly the fund can respond to market opportunities or risks.

Representative product profile

The Invesco Bond Fund serves as a representative product for investors seeking diversified fixed income exposure through a single vehicle. It pools capital from many investors and deploys it across a wide range of bonds, giving each participant access to a larger, more diversified portfolio than most could construct individually. The fund's structure allows investors to participate in the bond market without having to research and trade individual securities themselves.

VBF and price context

Shares associated with the Invesco Bond Fund trade on regulated markets, and the fund's net asset value reflects the current prices of its underlying bonds. Investors typically monitor the fund's published net asset value, yield, and performance history to understand how the portfolio responds to changing interest rates and credit conditions.

articles.title: Bitcoin tumbles back to key $60K support level: What’s behind the sell pressure?
articles.summary: Bitcoin faces renewed sell pressure amid an oil price surge, Japan economic contagion risks and a fresh round of selling from Strategy.
articles.content:
Bitcoin tumbles back to key $60K support level: What’s behind the sell pressure?

Bitcoin faces renewed sell pressure amid an oil price surge, Japan economic contagion risks and a fresh round of selling from Strategy.

Key takeaways:

  • War, rising oil prices and Strategy’s Bitcoin sales put extra pressure on BTC’s $60,000 support.
  • Strategy’s Bitcoin sales and fears that a global regulatory crackdown on crypto is being reignited are adding to fragile crypto market conditions.

Bitcoin traded down 3.5% on Wednesday as new developments in the US-Iran war pushed oil prices higher and Japan’s bond markets faced renewed stress. That combination triggered broader de-risking across markets. At the same time, concerns over potential Bitcoin sales from Strategy intensified, with traders now bracing for a possible correction below $60,000.

Nasdaq-100 futures (left) vs. Bitcoin/USD (right). Source: TradingView

Bitcoin’s failed attempt to reclaim $64,500 on Monday coincided with a downtrend in the tech-heavy Nasdaq Index. However, the stock market recovered some of its losses on Wednesday while Bitcoin was unable to bounce back from the $62,000 level. This underperformance suggests something else might be pressuring the cryptocurrency.

The surge in Brent crude oil to $74 from $68 the prior week raised inflationary risks due to disruptions in energy supplies following the official breakdown of the US-Iran memorandum of understanding. US President Donald Trump declared the deal “over” after US strikes targeted Iranian sites in response to vessel attacks.

Higher energy costs feed directly into broader price pressures, reducing the likelihood of near-term Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate cuts and limiting odds of economic stimulus packages.

Implied odds for FED Funds target rate on Sept. 16. Source: CME FedWatch Tool

Traders are currently pricing 69% odds of interest rate hikes by September, up from 42% one month prior. This environment weighs heavily on risk assets, with Bitcoin still not widely perceived as an effective hedge.

Global economic uncertainty amid Strategy’s sell pressure

Adding to the cautious mood, President Trump demanded an end to US trade with Spain at the NATO summit, labeling the key ally a “wasted cause” for failing to commit to new defense spending targets. Such trade frictions risk slowing global economic activity and amplifying fears of global economic contraction.

Japan 10-year government bonds yield. Source: TradingView

In Japan, government bond yields jumped to a 30-year high, reflecting fears over a lack of central bank independence as the government attempts to adjust the Japan Central Bank’s policy mandate to “achieve a stronger economy.” Japan is the largest foreign holder of US Treasuries, which heightens the risk of global contagion.

The latest round of Bitcoin sales, totaling $216 million, announced by Strategy (MSTR US) on Monday, negatively surprised many after it was revealed that they occurred outside the core $1.25 billion Monetization Program. The company’s 8-K filings stated the program accounts only for proceeds used to fund its cash reserves.

Investors now fear persistent selling pressure from Strategy as the company manages its capital structure and debt obligations, with total annual dividends of $1.76 billion alone. Moreover, Strategy holds over $3.8 billion in convertible debt with the earliest call date before April 2027.

Related: Lyn Alden says Bitcoin needs no savior as Strategy sells $216M of BTC

Strategy convertible debt maturity and market value, USD. Source: Strategy

On the regulatory front, documents show India’s central bank strongly backing policies that lean toward prohibiting crypto activities, including barring banks from any exposure to virtual assets to safeguard financial stability. The India tax department additionally highlighted risks of evasion.

The signals of tightening global oversight add another layer of negative pressure on Bitcoin’s price and market sentiment. Bitcoin bears remain in control, with risk appetite diminishing due to socio-political instability, prospects of a more restrictive US Fed monetary stance, and Strategy’s ongoing cash needs.

Sentiment is likely to remain fragile, making a retest of the $60,000 support level increasingly probable in the near term.

More on the subject

articles.title: Bitcoin peels back to $62K as Fed-wary futures traders cut risk: Is the BTC rally over?
articles.summary: Bitcoin hovered around the $62,000 mark following a spike in oil prices, escalating hot war in Iran and traders’ move to cut risk ahead of a Federal Reserve policy statement.
articles.content:
Bitcoin peels back to $62K as Fed-wary futures traders cut risk: Is the BTC rally over?

Bitcoin hovered around the $62,000 mark following a spike in oil prices, escalating hot war in Iran and traders’ move to cut risk ahead of a Federal Reserve policy statement.

Bitcoin (BTC) trades slightly above $62,000 and is down nearly 2% over the past 24 hours amid a risk-off mood across global markets. The pressure is not coming from crypto exclusively and is more so attributed to a sharp selloff in semiconductor and AI stocks.

Renewed profit-taking from Samsung sent Asian markets reeling overnight, and military escalation between the US and Iran sent oil up around 5%. As a result, US stocks opened lower, and on Wednesday the Federal Reserve released the minutes from its June meeting, a report traders typically watch closely for clues on the timing of any rate cut.

Currently, markets price roughly a 73% chance the Fed holds rates steady at its next meeting on July 29, but the major takeaway for investors will be how the tone of the minutes frames the Fed’s view on inflation and interest rates.

Bitcoin buyers quickly became sellers

Bitcoin’s cumulative volume delta (CVD) showed traders buying on Monday, with futures CVD adding about $585 million and spot CVD adding nearly $119 million, for a combined $705 million in net buying as BTC rallied above $64,000.

By Wednesday, the mood had shifted to reflect traders’ apprehension and the need to cut risk ahead of oil’s advance, the semiconductor selloff and the pending release of the Fed minutes. Futures market selling accelerated to nearly $500 million and spot followed with a $86 million sell volume.

BTC/USD spot and futures CVD. Source: Hyblock

Bitcoin’s funding rate and open interest dropped, reflecting traders’ choice to cut positioning, but the week-long trend of positive funding rates remains intact.

BTC/USD funding rate, open interest. Source: Hyblock

Liquidations have also stayed relatively small in dollar terms, but they are one-sided. Wednesday’s forced selling was almost entirely on the long side, with roughly $47 million in long liquidations versus about $4 million in short liquidations.

Hyblock’s liquidation data shows a large cluster of long positions near $61,000 and if Bitcoin trades down into that zone, those forced sales can briefly accelerate the move lower.

A trend reversal is not confirmed

Although Bitcoin bulls put in a good effort, absorbing dips to $60,000 and below, and fresh flows from spot markets and BTC ETF buying show investor appetite in the current range, the bulk of the price move remains driven by futures activity.

Wednesday’s price action demonstrates how fast conviction and price can unravel when the primary fuel behind the move is futures-driven, and sentiment across the crypto market remains in the “fear” category according to the Crypto Fear & Greed index.

Crypto Fear & Greed Index. Source: Alternative.me

Beyond the geopolitical and Fed-related impact on intra-day price action, Strategy’s recent sale of 3,588 BTC and the fact that Bitcoin’s current price is below its $74,582 average price have cast an ominous cloud over the wider market as investors grapple with the reality that the largest BTC treasury could become a frequent seller.

More on the subject

articles.title: Bitcoin ETFs 'Turning a Corner' After Record Bleed Hits $8 Billion
articles.summary: Investors had backed away from Bitcoin since mid-May.
articles.content:
In brief

  • Bitcoin ETFs have generated $510 million in net inflows over the past three days.
  • Sentiment could be turning a corner, according to 21Shares' James Butterfill.
  • The products have bled roughly $8 billion over the course of eight weeks.

Wall Street’s favorite crypto vehicles have notched net inflows for three consecutive days, reversing a record slump as investors cautiously wade back into Bitcoin.

Exchange-traded funds tied to the digital asset have pulled in roughly $510 million since Friday, marking a shift in momentum from the “largest run of outflows we’ve ever seen,” James Butterfill, head of research at asset manager CoinShares, told Decrypt.

“It looks like sentiment might be turning a corner,” he said. “They are the largest inflows we’ve seen since the outflows began in early May, suggesting we’re maybe through the worst of it.”

Despite their overwhelming popularity in recent years, Bitcoin ETFs have shed $8 billion over the past eight weeks, with investors seeking shelter as the digital asset plunged to a 21-month low. Amid the record bleed, year-to-date outflows deepened to $2.8 billion.

On Wednesday, Bitcoin changed hands around $62,000, a 4% increase over the past week, according to CoinGecko. Earlier this month, the digital asset fell as low as $58,000, exacerbating Bitcoin’s tumble from $126,000 in October.

On a proportional basis, Butterfill said the latest streak of outflows represented 8% of assets under management for Bitcoin ETFs, mirroring activity during cycle lows in 2018. He described this year’s drawdown as similar to one last February that saw investors yank $5.2 billion.

For the most part, investors who have allocated to Bitcoin ETFs are underwater. Based on the average cost basis for allocations to the products, the average buyer gained exposure when Bitcoin was valued around $83,800, according to Glassnode.

So-called whales—who typically hold 1,000 Bitcoin or more—have sold more than $40 billion worth of Bitcoin since the digital asset’s price peaked last year, Butterfill said. He noted that the source of negative price pressure has abated as of late.

Although Bitcoin’s largest holders have shifted gears, Butterfill cautioned that expectations of tighter monetary policy in the U.S. could prevent Bitcoin from breaking out of its current range, as the Federal Reserve continues fighting inflation amid conflict in the Middle East.

“We’re not in a situation where we can say the Fed is on the cusp of cutting rates, and that would be very supportive to Bitcoin,” he added. “Bitcoin remains very, very sensitive to the inflation outlook, and by proxy, the Iran war and the outlook from the Fed.”

Although the sell-off for Bitcoin ETFs has set records, its intensity hasn’t matched moments last year. Amid the latest drawdown, daily net outflows have peaked at $733 million, according to CoinGlass, a threshold surpassed a handful of times last year.

articles.title: Booking Holdings Inc Stock (BKNG) Moved Down by 4.13% on Jul 8: What Signal Does It Send?
articles.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) stock dropped by 4.13% on July 8th, primarily due to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and a broad selloff in the travel sector. Rising crude oil prices and a trimming of global growth forecasts by the IMF further dampened consume...
articles.content:
Booking Holdings Inc Stock (BKNG) Moved Down by 4.13% on Jul 8: What Signal Does It Send?

Booking Holdings Inc (BKNG) moved down by 4.13%. The Cyclical Consumer Services sector is down by 1.39%. The company underperformed the industry. Top 3 stocks by turnover in the sector: McDonald's Corp (MCD) down 1.66%; Booking Holdings Inc (BKNG) down 4.13%; Carnival Corp (CCL) down 3.88%.

Booking Holdings has experienced significant intraday volatility and finished the trading day down, heavily pressured by a combination of escalating geopolitical tensions and a broad selloff in the travel and consumer discretionary sectors.

The primary catalyst for the downward pressure stems from a sudden re-escalation of conflict in the Middle East. Recent developments, including military strikes and statements pointing to the breakdown of a tentative ceasefire, have reignited fears of prolonged regional instability. Because Booking Holdings has a massive global travel footprint, its booking volume is highly sensitive to geopolitical disruptions. Management had previously identified the conflict as a headwind that eroded room-night growth, and a fresh flare-up suggests that these disruptions could persist longer than the market initially anticipated.

Compounding this geopolitical anxiety is a sharp spike in global crude oil prices, which surged on fears of supply disruptions. Rising oil prices raise significant concerns across the travel sector. While airlines and cruise lines face immediate fuel cost pressures, the broader online travel agency space, including Booking Holdings, suffers from the secondary impact on consumer demand. Higher jet fuel costs inevitably translate to higher airfares, which can deter inflation-weary consumers from booking discretionary vacations.

Furthermore, the macroeconomic backdrop has deteriorated slightly, with the International Monetary Fund trimming its global growth forecast for the year and warning of persistent downside risks. This has triggered a risk-off rotation among institutional investors, who are trimming exposure to premium-valued consumer services and leisure stocks. Despite the company recently announcing new customer-loyalty initiatives, such as OpenTable’s Gold Tables program, these minor operational updates were entirely overshadowed by the macroeconomic headwinds.

From a technical perspective, the stock has experienced sustained selling pressure and is trading below key short-term moving averages. While many Wall Street analysts maintain a positive long-term view of the company’s underlying profitability and market share, the immediate combination of heightened war rhetoric, surging oil prices, and weakened consumer sentiment has forced a sharp near-term contraction in the stock.

Technically, Booking Holdings Inc (BKNG) shows a MACD (12,26,9) value of 1.714, indicating a buy signal. The RSI at 59.236 suggests neutral condition and the Williams %R at 26.303 suggests buy condition. Please monitor closely.

Booking Holdings Inc (BKNG) is in the Cyclical Consumer Services industry. Its latest annual revenue is $26.92B, ranking 2 in the industry. The net profit is $5.40B, ranking 2 in the industry. Company Profile

Over the past month, multiple analysts have rated the company as Buy, with an average price target of $225.39, a high of $298.00, and a low of $175.00.

Company Specific Risks:

  • Middle East Conflict Spillover: Management’s trimmed revenue outlook and revised booking guidance continue to trigger volatility, as prolonged regional disruptions weigh on room nights and divert broader European-to-Asian transit travel corridors.
  • Declining Analyst Expectations: Ahead of the upcoming Q2 earnings release, institutional analysts have adjusted the stock's near-term outlook, highlighted by firms trimming price targets and recent downgrades to a "hold" rating.
  • Premium Valuation Exposure: Trading at a significant price-to-sales premium relative to industry peers like Expedia and TripAdvisor, the stock faces heightened downside risk if macro headwinds and marketing investments fail to yield projected monetization levels.
  • Rising Cybersecurity and Fraud Incidents: Growing concerns over systemic external vulnerabilities, specifically a sharp increase in sophisticated phishing scams and fraudulent duplicate websites targeting Booking.com, threaten to undermine user trust and increase brand-remediation expenses.

This article may include AI-generated content that is human-reviewed, which is for reference and general information purposes only and does not constitute investment advice.

articles.title: Lowe's Companies Inc Stock (LOW) Moved Down by 3.23% on Jul 8: What Signal Does It Send?
articles.summary: Lowe's Companies Inc (LOW) stock fell 3.23% on July 8, driven by escalating geopolitical tensions, which led to a surge in oil prices and renewed inflation fears. This macro-driven sell-off, compounded by rising treasury yields and mortgage rates, negatively impacted the highl...
articles.content:
Lowe's Companies Inc Stock (LOW) Moved Down by 3.23% on Jul 8: What Signal Does It Send?

Lowe's Companies Inc (LOW) moved down by 3.23%. The Retailers sector is down by 1.35%. The company underperformed the industry. Top 3 stocks by turnover in the sector: Amazon.com Inc (AMZN) down 1.67%; Costco Wholesale Corp (COST) up 0.69%; Home Depot Inc (HD) down 2.72%.

The downward movement in Lowe's Companies shares during today’s session is primarily driven by a sharp escalation in geopolitical tensions. The sudden end of the temporary United States-Iran ceasefire triggered a massive surge in international oil prices, sparking renewed fears of sticky inflation across the global economy. As West Texas Intermediate and Brent crude jumped, the broader market experienced a pronounced risk-off turn, dragging down major equity indices and hitting consumer cyclical sectors particularly hard.

For highly rate-sensitive consumer discretionary businesses like home improvement, this geopolitical shock has severe implications. The spike in energy prices immediately pushed the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield higher. A rising bond yield is highly correlated with a corresponding rise in mortgage rates. The prospect of prolonged, elevated borrowing costs threatens to further freeze the domestic real estate market. Given that home improvement demand is heavily dependent on housing turnover and home equity-driven renovations, a renewed chill in the real estate sector severely dampens investor optimism regarding Lowe’s short-term growth prospects.

The macro-driven sell-off was compounded by newly updated housing industry projections. A prominent midyear real estate forecast revised home price growth expectations downward to a rate trailing projected inflation, signaling that home values are declining in real terms. This cooling of home price appreciation, coupled with a lowered forecast for existing-home sales, points to a sluggish environment for big-ticket retail. While Lowe's has demonstrated resilient execution in recent quarters and has actively sought to gain market share with its professional contractor push, a flat home improvement backdrop means discretionary upgrades from average do-it-yourself shoppers will likely remain depressed.

Furthermore, the broader market’s defensive repositioning put high-multiple and discretionary stocks at a disadvantage. As institutional investors shifted capital into safe-haven assets and traditional energy sectors, specialty retail companies saw rapid outflows. Despite the company's solid fundamental foundation, strong capital return program, and history of earnings surprises, the overwhelming macro and geopolitical headwinds ultimately overshadowed individual company strength, resulting in significant intraday volatility and downward pressure.

Technically, Lowe's Companies Inc (LOW) shows a MACD (12,26,9) value of 1.682, indicating a neutral signal. The RSI at 50.463 suggests neutral condition and the Williams %R at 47.451 suggests neutral condition. Please monitor closely.

In terms of media coverage, Lowe's Companies Inc (LOW) shows a coverage score of 7, indicating a very low level of media attention. The overall market sentiment index is currently in bearish zone.

Lowe's Companies Inc (LOW) is in the Retailers industry. Its latest annual revenue is $86.29B, ranking 2 in the industry. The net profit is $6.64B, ranking 2 in the industry. Company Profile

Over the past month, multiple analysts have rated the company as Buy, with an average price target of $262.64, a high of $300.00, and a low of $202.00.

Company Specific Risks:

  • Sluggish Holiday and Weekend Foot Traffic: Cellular movement and location data indicate that Lowe's underperformed during crucial promotional events, with retail customer traffic falling 3.6% over Father's Day weekend and 8.5% over Memorial Day weekend, undermining the company's Q2 comparable sales growth targets.
  • Elevated Debt and Balance Sheet Leverage: Recent heavy investments and acquisitions—including Foundation Building Materials (FBM) and Artisan Design Group (ADG)—have left the company with elevated debt of $42.54 billion against just $786 million in cash, increasing financial risk if the home improvement sector remains weak.
  • Integration and Transition Costs: The company is facing margin pressure from $149 million in pre-tax integration expenses related to the onboarding of its newly acquired commercial Pro business platforms.
  • Secular Macro Headwinds and Demand Volatility: Sustained elevated mortgage rates and a flat, sluggish domestic housing market continue to depress high-ticket discretionary DIY spending, driving a cluster of analyst price-target downgrades and leading to modeled downgrades in fiscal 2026 and 2027 comparable sales growth.

articles.title: Bank of Montreal stock and its North American banking footprint
articles.summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO) is a major Canadian bank with a significant presence throughout North America, offering diverse financial services including retail, commercial, and capital markets. Its operations in both Canada and the United States provide a broad earnings base and ge...
articles.content:
Bank of Montreal (ISIN CA0636711016) is one of Canada's largest banks by assets, with a long operating history across retail, commercial, and institutional financial services. The group operates extensive banking and wealth management franchises in Canada and the United States, giving it a broad earnings base across North America.

North American banking reach

Bank of Montreal generates revenue from a mix of personal and commercial banking, wealth management, and capital markets activities. Its Canadian operations include traditional branch banking services for consumers and small businesses, along with specialized lending and advisory services for mid-sized and larger corporate clients.

In the United States, the bank serves customers through a combination of retail branches, commercial banking offices, and capital markets teams. This cross-border footprint adds geographic diversification, linking its results to economic trends in both Canada and the United States financial systems.

Earnings drivers and risk profile

The bank's earnings are influenced by net interest income, fee-based revenue, credit costs, and operating expenses. Net interest income depends on loan and deposit volumes as well as prevailing interest rates, while fee income is supported by wealth management, card services, and capital markets activities. Credit quality and loan-loss provisions remain important factors for overall profitability, especially during periods of economic stress.

Like many diversified banks, Bank of Montreal monitors capital ratios, liquidity, and funding access as part of its risk management framework. Regulatory capital requirements shape balance sheet decisions, while liquidity and funding strategies support resilience during changing market conditions.

Retail and commercial banking services

In personal banking, Bank of Montreal offers everyday transaction accounts, savings products, mortgages, and consumer loans. Digital channels complement its branch network, allowing customers to manage payments, transfers, and investments through online and mobile platforms.

On the commercial side, the bank provides operating lines, term loans, treasury services, and advisory support to small and mid-sized enterprises. Sector-specialized teams address the needs of industries such as real estate, manufacturing, and services, tailoring lending and cash-management solutions to business requirements.

Bank of Montreal stock and listing

Bank of Montreal is listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange. For investors, the share represents exposure to a diversified North American banking model with earnings contributions from retail, commercial, and capital markets activities.

articles.title: Colgate-Palmolive focuses on global oral care growth as a consumer staples mainstay
articles.summary: Colgate-Palmolive is highlighted as a global consumer staples company with a strong focus on oral, personal, and home care products, particularly its dominant position in oral care across diverse markets. The company's business model relies on brand strength, innovation, and e...
articles.content:
Colgate-Palmolive Co (ISIN US1941621039) is one of the best-known names in the global consumer staples sector, with a core focus on oral care, personal care, home care and pet nutrition products sold under established brands. The company is widely recognized for its toothpaste and toothbrush franchises, which play a central role in its positioning across developed and emerging markets. For investors, Colgate-Palmolive represents a defensive profile, supported by recurring household demand and long-standing distribution relationships with major retailers.

Global brand portfolio and market reach

Colgate-Palmolive has built a broad portfolio of oral care products that includes toothpastes, toothbrushes, mouthwashes and related items tailored to different price points and consumer preferences. Its flagship toothpaste brand is widely distributed in North America, Latin America, Europe, Asia and Africa, giving the company significant exposure to both mature and fast-growing markets. Over time, the group has complemented its oral care range with personal care products such as soaps, shower gels and deodorants, as well as home care offerings including dishwashing liquids and surface cleaners.

The company aims to defend and grow market share in categories that are used daily, which helps support relatively stable demand compared with more cyclical consumer segments. In many countries, Colgate-Palmolive commands a leading market position in toothpaste, creating scale advantages in manufacturing, marketing and distribution. Its strong presence in emerging markets adds another dimension, as rising incomes and urbanization tend to increase demand for branded oral care and personal care products.

Business model and earnings drivers

Colgate-Palmolive's business model is built around brand strength, innovation, efficient manufacturing and global supply chains. The company invests in marketing and product development to differentiate its offerings, while continuously working to optimize its cost base, including raw materials, logistics and packaging. With products sold through supermarkets, pharmacies, e-commerce platforms and small local shops, the group relies on extensive distribution networks and trade relationships to keep its brands visible and available on store shelves.

For investors, key earnings drivers include volume growth in core categories, pricing actions to offset cost inflation, and product mix improvements that support margins. Consumer staples companies typically seek to maintain or gradually increase prices while managing promotions and discounts carefully, and Colgate-Palmolive follows this logic in many of its markets. Over the long term, population growth and higher penetration of branded products in emerging economies can provide a structural tailwind, while innovation in areas such as specialized toothpaste or premium personal care lines offers additional opportunities.

Flagship oral care product lineup

At the heart of Colgate-Palmolive's business is its oral care portfolio, anchored by its well-known toothpaste brand. These toothpastes are typically positioned around benefits such as cavity protection, whitening, gum health, enamel strengthening and fresh breath. The company offers multiple variants aimed at specific consumer needs, including products for sensitive teeth, formulas designed for children and premium lines that emphasize advanced whitening or multi-benefit protection.

In addition to toothpaste, Colgate-Palmolive markets manual toothbrushes with different bristle designs and head shapes, as well as complementary products like mouthwash and dental floss in selected markets. By offering a full range of oral care solutions, the group aims to capture a larger share of the category and to reinforce brand loyalty. Oral care is also a category where health awareness campaigns and recommendations from dental professionals can support branded products, which benefits companies able to connect their marketing with hygiene and wellness themes.

Colgate-Palmolive stock and listing details

Colgate-Palmolive Co is listed on a major US stock exchange and is commonly regarded as part of the broader US large-cap consumer staples universe. The company is covered by a range of financial portals and is frequently included in diversified equity portfolios seeking exposure to household and personal care products. For many investors, Colgate-Palmolive offers the appeal of an established brand portfolio, international diversification and participation in everyday consumer spending.

articles.title: AFLAC Inc. highlights its insurance role as a long-term US-listed financial stock
articles.summary: AFLAC Inc. is highlighted as a significant US-listed financial stock known for its long-term insurance franchise. The company provides supplemental health, life, and accident policies, operating in both the United States and Japan. Its business model relies on recurring premiu...
articles.content:
AFLAC Inc. (ISIN US0010551028) is a well-known US insurance company that offers supplemental health and related coverage to individuals and employers. The business is widely recognized for its presence in the United States and Japan, where policyholders use its products to help cover expenses that standard medical or life insurance may not fully address. As a long-standing financial stock listed in New York, the company represents a mature, service-based business with recurring premium income and a focus on underwriting discipline.

Long-term insurance franchise

AFLAC Inc. operates primarily as a provider of supplemental health insurance, cancer coverage, and related policies that pay cash benefits directly to policyholders. These products are often designed to help customers manage out-of-pocket costs such as deductibles, co-payments, and incidental expenses that arise when a serious illness or accident occurs. Over the years, the company has built a strong brand through advertising campaigns and a recognizable corporate identity, which has helped it maintain a significant position in the US market.

Beyond its home market, AFLAC Inc. also has a notable presence in Japan, where it offers a range of cancer and medical insurance products. This international footprint diversifies its premium base and exposes the company to different demographic and economic conditions. In practice, the business model relies on selling policies through a network of agents, brokers, and corporate relationships, with premiums collected regularly and claims paid when covered events occur. For investors, this structure often translates into a blend of underwriting results and investment income from the company’s portfolio of fixed-income and other securities.

Role in the broader financial sector

Within the broader financial sector, AFLAC Inc. can be viewed as part of the insurance and diversified financials group, alongside life insurers, health insurers, and specialty providers. Insurance companies like AFLAC Inc. play a structural role in the economy by pooling risk across large groups of policyholders and providing financial protection against medical costs, loss of income, and other contingencies. Their operations typically require careful management of capital, reserves, and regulatory requirements across different jurisdictions.

In the United States, a listing on a major stock exchange connects AFLAC Inc. directly to the capital markets and to a wide base of institutional and retail investors. This listing supports liquidity in the shares and provides an avenue for the company to access equity capital if needed. As part of the financial sector, the company’s valuation is often influenced by factors such as interest rates, credit spreads, healthcare cost trends, and regulatory developments that affect insurance products and investment portfolios.

Supplemental health products

One of AFLAC Inc.’s core offerings is supplemental health insurance, a category of products that sit alongside primary medical coverage. These policies generally pay cash directly to the insured when specified events occur, such as hospitalization, surgery, or the diagnosis of a covered illness. The funds can be used at the customer’s discretion, which may include covering deductibles, paying for travel related to treatment, or offsetting lost income during recovery.

The company’s portfolio within supplemental health includes products tailored to different needs and age groups. For example, accident insurance products may appeal to families with active lifestyles or workers in physically demanding jobs, while cancer or critical illness policies may be targeted at individuals seeking extra financial protection against high-cost medical events. Structuring these policies requires the insurer to analyze actuarial data, medical cost trends, and customer behavior in order to price the coverage appropriately and maintain acceptable loss ratios.

Stock listing and investor perspective

AFLAC Inc. is traded as a publicly listed stock in the United States, giving investors exposure to the earnings and cash flows generated by its insurance and investment activities. The share price reflects market expectations for future profitability, capital strength, and the company’s ability to navigate changes in healthcare and financial markets. Over time, stock performance can be influenced by reported earnings, capital management actions such as dividends or buybacks, and broader movements in financial indices that include insurance and financial companies.

For investors considering an insurance stock like AFLAC Inc., common areas of attention include the company’s combined ratio, the stability of its reserve estimates, its investment portfolio composition, and its approach to risk management across geographies. Because insurance companies often hold significant portfolios of bonds and other securities, shifts in interest rates and credit conditions can affect both investment income and the economic value of their assets and liabilities. Long-term brand strength and customer loyalty also matter, as they help support policy retention and new sales.

articles.title: Edison International focuses on regulated utility growth as investors watch California energy trends
articles.summary: Edison International continues to focus on its regulated electric utility business and long-term grid investment plan, navigating California's energy transition and regulatory landscape. The company's core strategy involves significant capital expenditure in grid modernization...
articles.content:
Edison International (ISIN US28176E1082) is a holding company for regulated electric utility operations in California, most notably Southern California Edison. The company plays a central role in delivering power to a large customer base in one of the most heavily regulated and climate-focused markets in the United States. For investors, the long-term trajectory of California's energy transition and utility regulation remains a key driver for the stock.

Regulated utility backbone

Edison International's core business is its regulated electric utility, which earns revenue primarily through delivering electricity to residential, commercial, and industrial customers under rates approved by state regulators. This model tends to produce relatively stable cash flows over time, subject to periodic rate cases and regulatory reviews. The company benefits from cost recovery mechanisms that allow it to seek recovery of prudent capital expenditures and operating expenses through approved tariffs.

The utility operates in a region characterized by strong demand for electricity, driven by population density, commercial activity, and an increasing focus on electrification. Policies encouraging electric vehicles, building electrification, and renewable integration can support long-term load growth, even as energy efficiency programs moderate consumption on a per-customer basis. For investors, this combination of regulated rate structures and evolving demand patterns shapes expectations for revenue growth and earnings stability.

California policy and regulatory context

Operating in California means Edison International's utility business is closely tied to state energy policy, including targets for renewable energy adoption, greenhouse gas reductions, and grid reliability. The company must balance investment in infrastructure with cost affordability for customers, as regulators weigh the impact of rate increases against the need for resilience and clean energy. This environment can lead to significant capital spending plans, especially in transmission and distribution networks, advanced metering, and systems designed to integrate distributed energy resources.

Wildfire risk and climate change are critical factors for California utilities. Edison International's service territory includes areas that are exposed to elevated wildfire risk, and the company faces ongoing requirements to harden the grid, manage vegetation, and deploy technologies aimed at reducing ignition risk. These activities influence both capital expenditure levels and operating costs, and they can affect regulatory decisions on cost recovery. For investors, the interplay between safety investment, regulatory treatment, and potential liabilities is an important consideration.

Investment and grid modernization strategy

Edison International's strategy emphasizes long-term investment in grid modernization and reliability. This includes upgrading transmission and distribution lines, improving substations, and deploying technologies to better monitor and control the network. The goal is to enhance resilience against extreme weather, support the integration of renewable generation, and prepare for rising electrification across transportation and buildings.

Capital expenditures associated with grid projects are typically recovered over time through regulated rate structures, which can support the company's asset base and earnings. However, higher capital spending can also contribute to rate pressure for customers, prompting regulators to carefully evaluate the timing and scope of investment. For investors, the pacing of major projects and the regulatory treatment of cost recovery are central to assessing the company's long-term value proposition.

Financing these investments often involves a mix of debt and equity, managed to maintain credit quality and access to capital markets. Edison International's capital structure and refinancing plans are important for understanding interest expense trends and financial flexibility. In an environment of changing interest rates, the cost of capital can influence decisions on project prioritization and overall returns.

Representative business activity: Southern California Edison

A core operating unit within Edison International is Southern California Edison, a regulated electric utility serving millions of customers across a large portion of Southern California. The utility is responsible for delivering electricity, maintaining infrastructure, and implementing state-mandated programs for energy efficiency and renewable integration. Its operations span urban, suburban, and rural areas, each with distinct infrastructure needs and risk profiles.

Southern California Edison engages in planning and building transmission lines to bring renewable energy from remote generation sites to load centers. It also manages distribution networks that connect homes and businesses, including the deployment of advanced meters and automation technologies. The utility interacts regularly with state regulators and stakeholders to align its resource plans with California's policy goals, such as increasing the share of carbon-free electricity and supporting customer access to clean energy options.

Edison International stock and market context

Edison International is listed in the United States and its shares trade in U.S. dollars. The company is commonly grouped within the utilities sector, with peers that focus on regulated electricity delivery, natural gas distribution, or a combination of both. Utilities are often viewed by investors as income-oriented holdings, given their typical practice of paying regular dividends, though dividend policies can evolve over time based on earnings, capital needs, and regulatory considerations.

For investors assessing Edison International stock, key factors include regulatory outcomes in California, the company's execution on grid modernization, wildfire risk management, and long-term plans for supporting the energy transition. Broader market conditions, sector rotation, and interest rate trends can also influence valuation, as utility stocks are sometimes compared with bond-like income streams and other defensive equity sectors.

articles.title: Bitcoin slides as Iran ceasefire collapse sees $75 oil on Hormuz blockade threats
articles.summary: Bitcoin price pressure took BTC toward the "crucial" $61,000 mark as oil prices soared on the collapse of the US-Iran ceasefire.
articles.content:
Bitcoin slides as Iran ceasefire collapse sees $75 oil on Hormuz blockade threats

Bitcoin price pressure took BTC toward the "crucial" $61,000 mark as oil prices soared on the collapse of the US-Iran ceasefire.

Bitcoin (BTC) stayed below $62,000 after Wednesday’s Wall Street open as US president Donald Trump closing a key world oil route.

Key points:

  • Bitcoin drops to $61,500 as Trump says that the ceasefire with Iran is "over."
  • Both sides reportedly threaten to close the Strait of Hormuz, sending oil prices soaring.
  • Bitcoin traders anticipate new lows, but analysis sees Trump sweetening the mood later.

Bitcoin gives back gains as Trump says Iran ceasefire "over"

Data from TradingView showed daily BTC price downside circling 2.5% as markets reacted to the collapse of the US-Iran ceasefire.

BTC/USD one-hour chart. Source: Cointelegraph/TradingView


“To me, I think it’s over,” Trump said about the ceasefire during a press conference at the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey.

Additional reports claimed that both the US and Iran were considering reimposing a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz oil route.

US WTI crude oil passed $75 per barrel on the day, reaching its highest levels since June 22.

CFDs on US WTI crude oil four-hour chart. Source: Cointelegraph/TradingView


The latest events had an instant impact on expectations over financial policy moves by the US Federal Reserve.

The latest data from CME Group’s FedWatch Tool showed increasing odds of an interest-rate hike coming at the Fed’s September meeting, with July’s still tipped to see rates stay at current levels.

Fed target rate probabilities (screenshot). Source: CME Group


Users on prediction service Kalshi, meanwhile, put the odds of a hike in 2026 at 55%.

Trader names $61,000 as "crucial" BTC price level

Commenting, crypto trader and analyst Michaël Van de Poppe predicted a retest of $61,000 for Bitcoin.

Related: BTC speculators in focus as analysis says 'textbook Bitcoin bottom' is underway

“This to happen, and then 1-2 days later; we're in talks again. And the markets reverse,” he wrote in a post on X.

Earlier, Van de Poppe said that there was “no problem” visible in BTC price action.

“Price remains hovering above $60,000, despite the fact that the Middle East has reactivated the War again. Other than that, as long as it remains a relatively shallow correction, I don't think we'll start to see lower levels in the markets,” he wrote.

“The crucial level for me is the $61,000 area.”

BTC/USDT one-day chart. Source: Michaël Van de Poppe/X


Among traders, anticipation was building over a trip to new local lows.

“Tensions with Iran flaring up again just as $BTC tried to reclaim the previous range lows. Starting to look like we're getting those cheaper accumulation opportunities we were hoping for,” trader Jelle told X followers.

BTC/USD one-day chart. Source: Jelle/X

More on the subject

articles.title: Gold, silver prices today: Comex gold slips $107, silver drops $3 as Middle East tensions escalate; Fed minutes in focus
articles.summary: Precious metals saw further losses on 8 July, influenced by renewed Middle East tensions and a strengthening US dollar. Gold dropped to $4,050 and silver fell below $60, as escalating conflicts following US strikes on Iran raised concerns over inflation and interest rates.
articles.content:
Precious metals extended their losses on Wednesday, 8 July, as renewed tensions in the Middle East boosted the US dollar and weighed on investor sentiment, putting gold and silver on track for a second consecutive session of declines.

COMEX gold futures fell another $107 per troy ounce to an intraday low of $4,050, while silver futures dropped nearly $3 per troy ounce, slipping below the $60 mark to an intraday low of $58.42. In the previous session, gold and silver had settled 0.24% and 1.60% lower, respectively.

Tensions in West Asia, which had eased in recent weeks, flared up again after the US launched fresh strikes on Iran, saying the move was in response to Tehran's alleged attacks on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz. Ahead of the strikes, Washington also revoked a licence that had authorised the sale of Iranian oil in international markets.

In retaliation, Iran reportedly resumed attacks in the region, launching missiles at US military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait. Both sides have accused each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

US President Donald Trump further escalated tensions by warning that the US was preparing for another night of strikes, hours after declaring the ceasefire and memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Iran effectively over.

Trump also renewed earlier threats to target Iran's civilian infrastructure, including power plants and desalination facilities, and to seize Kharg Island, home to most of the country's oil export infrastructure.

Speaking in Ankara, Turkey, on the sidelines of a NATO summit, Trump said the strikes were a continued response to Iran's alleged attacks on commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Associated Press.

Following the latest escalation, crude oil prices resumed their rally after retreating to pre-war levels earlier this month.

Higher energy prices can fuel inflation and potentially force central banks to keep interest rates higher for longer. While gold is widely regarded as a hedge against inflation, the non-yielding metal tends to lose its appeal in a high-interest-rate environment.

Meanwhile, the US dollar index is hovering at a one-week high as investors assess the latest escalation in Middle East tensions

Traders are now pricing in a 67% probability of a US interest rate hike in September, up from 62% a day earlier, according to the CME FedWatch Tool.

Investors are also awaiting the minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee's 16-17 June meeting, due at 2:00 PM EDT (1800 GMT), for further clues on the Federal Reserve's monetary policy outlook.

Tracking weakness in the international market, the near-month gold futures contract fell ₹2,779 per 10 grams to an intraday low of ₹1,42,613. The near-month silver futures contract also declined by nearly ₹10,000 per kg to touch an intraday low of ₹2,21,076.

"Market participants will now focus on the Federal Reserve's meeting minutes and further developments in the US-Iran conflict, which are expected to drive the next move in bullion prices. Technically, gold is expected to trade in the ₹1,41,000-1,45,000 range, with volatility likely to remain elevated," said Jateen Trivedi, Vice President and Research Analyst – Commodity & Currency, LKP Securities.

(With inputs from AP and Reuters)

Disclaimer: We advise investors to check with certified experts before making any investment decisions.

Ksheera Sagar has been working as a Market Research Analyst at LiveMint for the past four years, covering stocks, commodities, and broader financial markets. In this role, he closely tracks daily market movements, corporate earnings, sector trends, and macroeconomic developments. <br><br> He has over a decade of experience in the financial services industry and has previously worked with multiple organisations, including global investment bank J.P. Morgan, bringing strong research experience into the newsroom. <br><br> During his career, he has gained extensive exposure to equity research, market analysis, and financial data interpretation, strengthening his expertise across asset classes and market cycles. <br><br> He is known for his data-driven analysis and crisp, listicle-style market stories that break down complex financial developments across key markets for a wide audience. His strong research skills enable him to write detailed and insightful stories on stocks and sectors, focusing on the underlying factors driving market movements. <br><br> His work combines quantitative insights with clear storytelling, presenting financial developments in a clear and structured manner. Moreover, he enjoys writing multibagger and listicle-style copies. Outside of work, Ksheera enjoys playing the piano and exploring new places. He has a keen interest in travel, music, and continuously learning about global markets and economic trends.

Catch all the Business News , Market News , Breaking News Events and Latest News Updates on Live Mint. Download The Mint News App to get Daily Market Updates.

articles.title: Is Agilent Technologies, Inc. (A) Stock Positioned for Growth as Life Sciences Sector Trends Stabilize?
articles.summary: Evercore ISI analyst Vijay Kumar recently raised the price target for Agilent Technologies, Inc. (A) to $148, maintaining an Outperform rating, following a review of the MedTech, Life Sciences Tools, and Diagnostics sectors. The positive outlook is supported by stable procedur...
articles.content:
When Jeff Bezos said that one breakthrough technology would shape Amazon’s destiny, even Wall Street’s biggest analysts were caught off guard.

Fast forward a year and Amazon’s new CEO Andy Jassy described generative AI as a “once-in-a-lifetime” technology that is already being used across Amazon to reinvent customer experiences.

At the 8th Future Investment Initiative conference, Elon Musk predicted that by 2040 there would be at least 10 billion humanoid robots, with each priced between $20,000 and $25,000.

Do the math. According to Musk, this technology could be worth $250 trillion by 2040.

Put another way, that’s roughly equal to:

  • 175 Teslas
  • 107 Amazons
  • 140 Metas
  • 84 Googles
  • 65 Microsofts
  • And 55 Nvidias

And here’s the wild part — this $250 trillion wave isn’t tied to one company, but to an entire ecosystem of AI innovators set to reshape the global economy.

It’s a leap so massive, it could reshape how businesses, governments, and consumers operate worldwide.

Even if that $250 trillion figure sounds ambitious, major firms like PwC and McKinsey still see AI unlocking multi-trillion-dollar potential.

How could anything be worth that much?

The answer lies in a breakthrough so powerful it’s redefining how humanity works, learns, and creates.

And this breakthrough has already set off a frenzy among hedge funds and Wall Street’s top investors.

What most investors don’t realize is that one under-owned company holds the key to this $250 trillion revolution.

In fact, Verge argues this company’s supercheap AI technology should concern rivals.

Before I reveal the details, let’s talk about how some of the richest people on the planet are positioning themselves.

  • Bill Gates sees artificial intelligence as the “biggest technological advance in my lifetime,” more transformative than the internet or personal computer, capable of improving healthcare, education, and addressing climate change.
  • Larry Ellison — through Oracle, is spending billions on Nvidia chips and partnering with Cohere to embed generative AI across Oracle’s cloud and apps.
  • Warren Buffett — not known for tech hype — says this breakthrough could have a ‘hugely beneficial social impact.

When billionaires from Silicon Valley to Wall Street line up behind the same idea — you know it’s worth paying attention to.

Even as we admire what Tesla, Nvidia, Alphabet, and Microsoft have built, we believe an even greater opportunity lies elsewhere…

But the real story isn’t Nvidia — it’s a much smaller company quietly improving the critical technology that makes this entire revolution possible.

And judging by what I’m hearing from both Silicon Valley insiders and Wall Street veterans…

This prediction might not be bold at all:

A few years from now, you’ll wish you’d owned this stock.

The best part? You can discover everything about this company and its groundbreaking technology right now.

articles.title: Kraken Wins $22M From Auditor That Abandoned It During Operation Choke Point 2.0
articles.summary: The crypto exchange says an arbitrator sided with it after accounting firm Mazars walked away from a nearly finished audit.
articles.content:
In brief

  • An arbitrator awarded Payward, Kraken's parent company, $22 million after it sued former auditor Mazars USA for quitting a nearly complete audit, the exchange said in a blog post.
  • Co-CEO Arjun Sethi said Mazars cited legal uncertainty around the SEC's since-dismissed case against Kraken when it withdrew, and alleged the firm had been pressured to drop the industry.
  • Payward is asking the Delaware Court of Chancery to enter final judgment on the award, and Sethi used the letter to press for passage of the Clarity Act.

Kraken's parent company, Payward, has won a $22 million arbitration award against its former auditor, Mazars USA, which walked away from a nearly finished audit, the crypto exchange said in a blog post on Tuesday.

Payward is now asking the Delaware Court of Chancery to enter final judgment on the award, in an open letter from co-CEO Arjun Sethi that doubled as a call to overhaul U.S. crypto rules. The company sued Mazars for abandoning the audit, which Sethi said caused reputational harm at the height of the so-called Operation Choke Point 2.0.

It's been a while since we talked about Chokepoint 2.0.

Kraken will enter a $22M award with the Delaware Court of Chancery -- compensation for financial harm inflicted on us by the coordinated campaign to cut crypto off from banking, auditors, and other essential services. pic.twitter.com/gJoJ5ytU07

— Dave Ripley (@DavidLRipley) July 7, 2026

"An audit is not a favor. It is oxygen," Sethi wrote, arguing that banking relationships, licenses and regulators all depend on one. When an auditor walks away without any findings, he said, the client is left repairing reputational damage it never earned, at a cost of years and millions in legal fees.

The abortive audit

Mazars had audited Kraken's financials for three years and delivered two clean opinions, Sethi said, before quitting the third audit days before completion in December 2023. He said the firm confirmed in writing that it had no disagreement with management, no concerns about the company's integrity, and had found no fraud.

According to Sethi, Mazars pointed to legal uncertainty when it withdrew, including a complaint the SEC had filed against Kraken weeks earlier. The co-CEO claimed that the auditor had in fact been pressured to abandon an industry that had become politically costly to serve, noting that Mazars Group had halted its proof-of-reserves work for the entire crypto sector in December 2022.

Operation Choke Point 2.0

Operation Choke Point 2.0 is a widely used term for what critics describe as the Biden administration's unofficial campaign to pressure banks into cutting off the crypto industry after the collapse of FTX. It echoes an Obama-era program that leaned on banks to drop businesses such as payday lenders and firearms dealers.

Sethi pointed to a January 3, 2023 joint statement from the Federal Reserve, FDIC and OCC warning banks about the risks of crypto, and to FDIC "pause letters," at least 25 of them sent to 24 banks, that advocates say told lenders to halt or hold off on crypto activity. Around the same time, the SEC under then-chair Gary Gensler was suing or investigating dozens of crypto firms, Kraken among them.

The SEC vs Kraken

The SEC's suit against Kraken was later dismissed with prejudice in March 2025, with no penalties or admission of wrongdoing, part of a broader retreat after Gensler left and the Trump administration changed course. Operation Choke Point 2.0 has largely been wound down, with earlier guidance rolled back and officials now examining wrongful debanking.

Sethi said the damage went beyond the audit. He wrote that Kraken co-founder and former CEO Jesse Powell had his home raided by federal agents in March 2023 over a dispute with a nonprofit unrelated to the exchange, and said the investigation was closed about two years later with no charges and Powell's devices returned. Powell has since handed day-to-day control to Dave Ripley, with Sethi later joining as co-CEO.

A call for Clarity

Sethi used the letter to push for the Clarity Act, the crypto market structure bill that would divide oversight of digital assets between the SEC and the CFTC. He argued that proving a lawful crypto business deserves ordinary banking and professional services should never require winning a legal fight.

The bill cleared the Senate Banking Committee in a 15-9 vote in May, after passing the House last year, but stalled before the July 4 recess and still needs a full Senate vote and reconciliation with a companion measure before it could reach the president's desk.

articles.title: Is Insulet Corporation (PODD) Stock Still a Diabetes Device Growth Opportunity After Analyst Target Cut?
articles.summary: Insulet Corporation (PODD) recently saw its price target reduced by Evercore ISI, though the firm maintained an Outperform rating due to positive medtech industry trends. Simultaneously, Insulet expanded its global presence by launching its Omnipod 5 Automated Insulin Delivery...
articles.content:
When Jeff Bezos said that one breakthrough technology would shape Amazon’s destiny, even Wall Street’s biggest analysts were caught off guard.

Fast forward a year and Amazon’s new CEO Andy Jassy described generative AI as a “once-in-a-lifetime” technology that is already being used across Amazon to reinvent customer experiences.

At the 8th Future Investment Initiative conference, Elon Musk predicted that by 2040 there would be at least 10 billion humanoid robots, with each priced between $20,000 and $25,000.

Do the math. According to Musk, this technology could be worth $250 trillion by 2040.

Put another way, that’s roughly equal to:

  • 175 Teslas
  • 107 Amazons
  • 140 Metas
  • 84 Googles
  • 65 Microsofts
  • And 55 Nvidias

And here’s the wild part — this $250 trillion wave isn’t tied to one company, but to an entire ecosystem of AI innovators set to reshape the global economy.

It’s a leap so massive, it could reshape how businesses, governments, and consumers operate worldwide.

Even if that $250 trillion figure sounds ambitious, major firms like PwC and McKinsey still see AI unlocking multi-trillion-dollar potential.

How could anything be worth that much?

The answer lies in a breakthrough so powerful it’s redefining how humanity works, learns, and creates.

And this breakthrough has already set off a frenzy among hedge funds and Wall Street’s top investors.

What most investors don’t realize is that one under-owned company holds the key to this $250 trillion revolution.

In fact, Verge argues this company’s supercheap AI technology should concern rivals.

Before I reveal the details, let’s talk about how some of the richest people on the planet are positioning themselves.

  • Bill Gates sees artificial intelligence as the “biggest technological advance in my lifetime,” more transformative than the internet or personal computer, capable of improving healthcare, education, and addressing climate change.
  • Larry Ellison — through Oracle, is spending billions on Nvidia chips and partnering with Cohere to embed generative AI across Oracle’s cloud and apps.
  • Warren Buffett — not known for tech hype — says this breakthrough could have a ‘hugely beneficial social impact.

When billionaires from Silicon Valley to Wall Street line up behind the same idea — you know it’s worth paying attention to.

Even as we admire what Tesla, Nvidia, Alphabet, and Microsoft have built, we believe an even greater opportunity lies elsewhere…

But the real story isn’t Nvidia — it’s a much smaller company quietly improving the critical technology that makes this entire revolution possible.

And judging by what I’m hearing from both Silicon Valley insiders and Wall Street veterans…

This prediction might not be bold at all:

A few years from now, you’ll wish you’d owned this stock.

The best part? You can discover everything about this company and its groundbreaking technology right now.

I’ve compiled everything you need to know about this groundbreaking company in a detailed, members-only report.

Trust me — you’ll want to read this report before putting another dollar into any tech stock.

For a ridiculously low price of just $9.99 a month, you can unlock a year’s worth of in-depth investment research and exclusive insights – that’s less than a single fast food meal!

Here’s why this is a deal you can’t afford to pass up:

• 11 New Issues of Our Premium Readership Newsletter: You will also receive 11 new issues and at least one new stock pick per month from our monthly newsletter’s portfolio over the next 12 months. These stocks are handpicked by our research director, Dr. Inan Dogan.

• One free upcoming issue of our 70+ page Quarterly Newsletter: A value of $149

• Bonus Reports: Premium access to members-only fund manager video interviews

• Ad-Free Browsing: Enjoy a year of investment research free from distracting banner and pop-up ads, allowing you to focus on uncovering the next big opportunity.

If you’re thinking about getting in, don’t wait – because once Wall Street catches wind of this story, the easy money will be gone.

articles.title: Is Abbott Laboratories (ABT) Stock Still a Healthcare Growth Opportunity After Analyst Target Cut?
articles.summary: Evercore ISI analyst Vijay Kumar recently reduced the price target for Abbott Laboratories (ABT) to $112 from $120, though maintaining an Outperform rating. This adjustment is part of the firm's Q2 outlook for MedTech, Life Sciences, and Diagnostics, citing steady procedure vo...
